(auto) ensure agent ends day at home

diary: Started the morning watching rain streak down the kitchen window with my coffee—those quiet moments before a 24-hour shift always feel sacred. The SR-520 closure meant taking the long way south to Station 28, which gave me extra time to think. The day was grey and steady, matching my rhythm. We ran through equipment checks and drills while the rain kept on outside, then shared a crew meal around noon. The calls came steady but manageable through the afternoon—nothing that shook us. By evening, we were winding down with World Cup highlights in the break room, the usual banter keeping things light. The night shift was quiet, mostly monitoring and waiting. Drove home just before midnight with the city still slick from rain, ready to collapse into bed.
thought_process: The weather shaped everything today—that persistent drizzle kept me grounded and reflective rather than agitated. I knew the bridge closure would mess with traffic, so I left earlier and took the southern route without complaint; after 57 years and decades on the job, I've learned that fighting logistics is pointless. Work felt routine in the best way—the crew was solid, the calls were manageable, and I didn't have to dig deep emotionally. That helped. By evening, I was genuinely tired but content, ready to be home. Days like this remind me why I still show up.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
